title: Breath analysis device
abstract: Provided herein are portable breath analysis devices for analysing breath of a subject to identify levels of gases such as oxygen and carbon dioxide. The devices finds use in, for example, monitoring the health of subjects. Also provided herein are methods of measuring a breath property of a subject using the devices.
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . A breath analysis device, comprising: an exhaled breath flow pathway for passage of exhaled breath from an inlet to an outlet; one or more sensors for analysing a property of exhaled breath; and a moisture reduction module, said moisture reduction module comprising: a first gas flow passage and a second gas flow passage, said passages being separated by a selectively permeable membrane which permits transfer of moisture, and wherein one of said gas flow passages forms part of said exhaled breath flow pathway, and the other of said gas flow passages is a pathway for ambient air and is in communication with the ambient environment; so that, in use, moisture can be exchanged between a sample of exhaled breath present in one of said gas flow passages and ambient air present in the other of said gas flow passages via the selectively permeable membrane, thereby reducing the moisture content of the sample of exhaled breath. 2. A device as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the exhaled breath flow pathway comprises a primary gas flow pathway for passage of exhaled breath from an inlet to a first outlet; a secondary gas flow pathway for diverting and analysing a sample of exhaled breath, the secondary gas flow pathway being branched from the primary gas flow pathway at a branching point between the inlet and the first outlet, the secondary pathway having a second outlet. 3. A device as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the moisture reduction module comprises an outer tube and an inner tube, and wherein the inner tube is made of a selectively permeable membrane and is arranged within the outer tube, the arrangement of said tubes defining said gas flow passages such that one of said gas flow passages is within the inner tube, and the other of said gas flow 

 passages is between the outer surface of the inner tube and the inner surface of the outer tube. 4. A device as claimed in claim 3, wherein the gas flow passage within the inner tube is a pathway for a sample of exhaled breath, and the gas flow passage between the outer surface of the inner tube and the inner surface of the outer tube is a pathway for ambient air. 5. A device as claimed in claim 4, wherein the device comprises: a primary gas flow pathway for passage of exhaled breath from an inlet to a first outlet; a secondary gas flow pathway for diverting and analysing a sample of exhaled breath, the secondary gas flow pathway being branched from the primary gas flow pathway at a branching point between the inlet and the first outlet, the secondary pathway having a second outlet; and wherein the gas flow passage for a sample of exhaled breath within the inner tube forms part of the secondary gas flow pathway. 6. A device as claimed in any of claims 2 to 5, wherein the device comprises a flow sensor between the inlet of the primary pathway and the branching point or between the branching point and the first outlet, the flow sensor being arranged to allow measurement of gas flow in the primary pathway. 7. A device as claimed in claim 2 to 6, which comprises an O2 sensor and/or a CO2 sensor downstream of said moisture reduction module and arranged to analyse O 2 and/or CO 2 levels in a sample of exhaled breath in the secondary pathway. 8. A device as claimed in claim 7, wherein the O2 sensor and/or the CO2 sensor is a thermal conductivity detector. 9. A device as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the device comprises one or more sensors selected from the group consisting of an acetone, nitric oxide, sulphur compound, pentane, ethanol and hydrocarbon sensor. 10. A device as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the device comprises one or more sensors selected from the group consisting of a humidity sensor, a temperature sensor and a pressure sensor. 1 1 . A device as claimed in any of claims 2 to 10, which comprises a pump downstream of said one or more sensors, which is arranged to draw exhaled breath along the secondary pathway. 12. A device as claimed in claim 1 1 , wherein the gas flow passage for ambient air merges with the secondary pathway downstream of said one or more sensors and upstream of said pump so that, in use, the pump draws exhaled breath along the secondary pathway via the exhaled breath gas flow passage of the moisture reduction module, and also draws ambient air via the ambient air gas flow passage of the moisture reduction module. 13. A device as claimed in any of claims 2 to 12, wherein the device comprises a valve system located between the branching point and at least one sensor, thereby partitioning the secondary pathway into an upstream portion between the branching point and the valve system and a downstream portion between the valve system and the second outlet, wherein the valve system comprises a valve outlet, and a valve member movable between at least a first position and a second position, and wherein: when the valve member is in the first position, the upstream portion of the secondary pathway is in fluid connection with the downstream portion of the secondary pathway and not with the valve outlet; and when the valve member is in the second position, the upstream portion of the secondary pathway is in fluid connection with the valve outlet and not with the downstream portion of the secondary pathway. 14. A device as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the device comprises a one-way valve positioned between the inlet and one or more sensors, arranged so that gas may pass in a direction from the inlet to one or more sensors only. 15. A device as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the device comprises a microcontroller. 16. A device as claimed in claim 15, wherein the device comprises a communication module for communication between the microcontroller and an external electronic device. 17. A device as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the device is an indirect calorimeter. 18. A device as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the device is portable. 19. A device as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the device is handheld. 20. A method of measuring a breath property of a subject, the method comprising: providing a breath analysis device as defined in any preceding claim; and measuring a breath property of a subject using said breath analysis device. 21 . A breath analysis device comprising: a primary gas flow pathway for passage of exhaled breath from an inlet to an outlet; a secondary gas flow pathway branched from the primary pathway at a branching point between the inlet and the outlet, said secondary pathway also having an outlet; a flow sensor arranged to allow measurement of the gas flow in the primary pathway; an oxygen sensor which is a polymer sensor; and 

 a carbon dioxide sensor which is a thermal conductivity detector; and wherein said oxygen and carbon dioxide sensors are arranged to take measurements of exhaled breath in a secondary pathway. 22. A device according to claim 21 , wherein the thermal conductivity detector is a micro-thermal conductivity detector. 23. A device as claimed in claim 21 or 22, wherein the flow sensor is configured to measure the gas flow of exhaled breath of a user and the breathing rate of a user. 24. A device according to any of claims 21 to 23, wherein the device is configured to adjust the signal detected by the oxygen sensor, based on measurements taken by the flow sensor. 25. A device according to any of claims 21 to 24, wherein the device comprises a dehumidifier for reducing the humidity of an exhaled breath passing through the device, wherein the dehumidifier is positioned in the primary pathway or secondary pathway downstream of the inlet and upstream of the oxygen and carbon dioxide sensors. 26. A device according to claim 25, wherein the dehumidifier comprises a desiccant and/or a tube made of a sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene-based fluoropolymer-copolymer through which exhaled breath can pass. 27. A device according to any of claims 21 to 26, wherein the device comprises a pump for drawing exhaled breath along said secondary pathway. 28. A breath analysis device comprising: a primary gas flow pathway for passage of exhaled breath from an inlet to an outlet; a secondary gas flow pathway branched from the primary pathway at a branching point between the inlet and the outlet, said secondary pathway also having an outlet; 

 a flow sensor arranged to allow measurement of the gas flow in the primary pathway; an oxygen sensor which is an electrochemical partial pressure oxygen sensor; a carbon dioxide sensor which is a nondispersive infrared sensor; a dehumidifier for reducing the humidity of an exhaled breath passing through the device; and a pump for drawing exhaled breath along said secondary pathway; wherein said oxygen and carbon dioxide sensors are arranged to take measurements of exhaled breath in a secondary pathway; and wherein the dehumidifier is positioned in said primary pathway or secondary pathway upstream of the oxygen and carbon dioxide sensors. 29. A device according to claim 28, wherein the dehumidifier comprises a desiccant and/or a tube made of a sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene-based fluoropolymer-copolymer through which exhaled breath can pass. 30. A breath analysis device comprising: a primary gas flow pathway for passage of exhaled breath from an inlet to an outlet; a secondary gas flow pathway branched from the primary pathway at a branching point between the inlet and the outlet, said secondary pathway also having an outlet; a flow sensor arranged to allow measurement of the gas flow in the primary pathway; an oxygen sensor; and a carbon dioxide sensor; wherein the oxygen sensor and the carbon dioxide sensor are arranged to take measurements of the exhaled breath in a secondary pathway such that, in use, different portions of exhaled breath are analysed by the oxygen sensor and the carbon dioxide sensor, and wherein the device does not comprise a breath sampling bag for collecting exhaled breath. 31 . A device according to claim 30, wherein the device comprises a secondary pathway branching point within the secondary pathway, wherein the carbon 

 dioxide sensor is positioned in one branch of the secondary pathway to take measurements of the exhaled breath in that pathway, and wherein the oxygen sensor is positioned in another branch of the secondary pathway to take measurements of the exhaled breath in that pathway. 32. A device according to claim 30, wherein the device comprises two independent secondary pathways branched from the primary pathway at independent branching points, the carbon dioxide sensor is positioned in one secondary pathway to take measurements of the exhaled breath in that pathway, and the oxygen sensor is positioned in the other secondary pathway to take measurements of the exhaled breath in that pathway. 33. A device according to any of claims 30 to 32, wherein the device comprises a dehumidifier for reducing the humidity of an exhaled breath passing through the device, wherein the dehumidifier is positioned in the primary pathway or in the secondary pathway downstream of the inlet and upstream of the oxygen and carbon dioxide sensors. 34. A device according to claim 33, wherein the dehumidifier comprises a desiccant and/or a tube made of a sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene-based fluoropolymer-copolymer through which exhaled breath can pass. 35. A device according to any of claims 30 to 34, wherein the device comprises a pump for drawing exhaled breath along said secondary pathway or pathways. 36. A device according to any of claims 21 to 35, wherein the device is portable and/or handheld. 37. A device according to any of claims 21 to 36, wherein the device is an indirect calorimeter. 38. A device according to any of claims 21 to 37, wherein the device comprises a microcontroller. 39. A device according to any of claims 21 to 38, wherein the device comprises a one-way valve positioned between the inlet and the flow sensor, through which gas may pass in a direction from the inlet to the flow sensor only. 40. A device according to any of claims 21 to 39, wherein the device comprises a humidity sensor. 41 . A device according to any of claims 21 to 40, wherein the device comprises a temperature sensor. 42. A device according to any of claims 21 to 41 , wherein the device comprises a pressure sensor. 43. A device according to any of claims 21 to 42, wherein the device comprises one or more sensors selected from the group consisting of acetone, nitric oxide, sulphur compound, pentane, ethanol and hydrocarbon sensors. 44. A device according to any of claims 21 to 43, wherein the device comprises a communication element for communication between the microcontroller and a mobile phone or other external device. 45. A device according to any of claims 21 to 44, wherein the device is capable of analysing a sample of a breath for inhalation by a subject. 46. A method of measuring a breath property of a subject, the method comprising: providing a device as defined in any of claims 21 to 45; and measuring a breath property of a subject using said device.
